subtopic_name: Claude Code CLI capabilities for deep research
summary: Claude Code is Anthropic's terminal CLI for Sonnet/Opus/Haiku that ships features purpose-built for multi-step research: subagents that isolate context (built-in Explore, Plan, general-purpose, plus user-defined ones in .claude/agents/), a Skills system at .claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md with YAML frontmatter and progressive-disclosure loading, slash commands unified with skills, lifecycle hooks driven from settings.json, MCP integration over stdio/HTTP/SSE via 'claude mcp add', built-in WebSearch and WebFetch tools, plan mode that restricts to read-only tools, and bundled skills like /loop and /batch for recurring or parallel work. Sonnet 4.6 and Opus 4.6 expose the full 1M-token context at standard pricing, making long financial document analysis tractable.

key_claims.claim: Claude Code subagents run in isolated context windows with their own system prompt, tool allowlist, and permissions, returning only a summary so the main conversation is not flooded with raw search results, logs, or file contents.
Claude Code ships built-in subagents named Explore, Plan, and general-purpose, and lets users define more in .claude/agents/ with optional model overrides for cost-aware routing to Haiku.

Skills live at ~/.claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md (personal) or .claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md (project), use YAML frontmatter with fields like description, allowed-tools, disable-model-invocation, context: fork, and agent, and unify with the older .claude/commands/ slash command files.

Skill descriptions plus when_to_use are truncated at 1,536 characters in the listing, and the overall character budget for skill listings scales at 1% of the context window with an 8,000-character fallback (overridable via SLASH_COMMAND_TOOL_CHAR_BUDGET).

Hooks are JSON entries in settings.json that fire on lifecycle events (PreToolUse, PostToolUse, UserPromptSubmit, SessionStart/End, Stop, SubagentStop, PreCompact, Notification, and more) and can run shell commands, HTTP calls, MCP tools, prompts, or spawn agents to block or modify tool calls.

Claude Code adds MCP servers via 'claude mcp add' supporting HTTP (recommended), stdio (local), and SSE (deprecated) transports, with three configuration scopes: local (default), project (shared via .mcp.json), and user (global).
Claude Code emits a warning when MCP tool output exceeds 10,000 tokens, and the cap is overridable via the MAX_MCP_OUTPUT_TOKENS environment variable.

WebSearch returns a small set of credible links and titles from Anthropic's server-side search; WebFetch downloads a known URL, converts HTML to Markdown via Turndown, truncates to roughly 100 KB of text, and answers a focused question against it rather than returning raw content.
Web search on the Claude API costs $10 per 1,000 searches plus standard token costs, while WebFetch has no per-call charge beyond the tokens of the fetched content.

Plan mode restricts Claude to read-only tools so it produces a reviewable plan artifact you must approve before any write or shell side-effects, and it pairs with Opus 4.6's 1M-token context for large-codebase planning.

Claude Sonnet 4.6 and Opus 4.6 both expose the full 1M-token context window at standard per-token pricing, with no surcharge above 200K input tokens (a change from the Sonnet 4.5 era where >200K input was billed at a premium).
Bundled skills include /simplify, /batch, /debug, /loop, and /claude-api, where /loop is a session-scoped scheduler that runs prompts on a recurring interval until the terminal session ends.

Skill content uses dynamic context injection: backtick-bang shell expressions like !`git diff HEAD` execute before the prompt is sent, so the skill arrives with live data already inlined, which is ideal for grounding stock-research prompts in fresh prices, filings, or news feeds.

limitations: Plan-mode primary docs page returned 403 so plan-mode details lean on a community-curated source (claudelog.com); exact MCP-registry server count was not extracted because the docs page renders the catalogue dynamically from api.anthropic.com/mcp-registry rather than printing a number.
